description: Roridulaceae is a monogeneric family of woody shrubs endemic to the Cape Floristic Region of South Africa. The genus Roridula produces sticky resin-coated leaves that trap insects, but lacks the digestive enzymes of true carnivores; instead, symbiotic assassin bugs (Pameridea spp.) consume prey and their frass fertilises the plant. Roridula represents a fascinating protocarnivorous or paracarnivorous strategy.
meta_description: Shop rare South African Roridula fly bushes. Protocarnivorous resin-leaved shrubs with unique insect symbiosis. TC stock with provenance.
meta_keywords: Roridula, fly bush, protocarnivorous plant, South Africa carnivore, buy Roridula

genera.description: Roridula comprises two species of resinous shrubs from the fynbos biome of the Western Cape, South Africa. Leaves are densely covered with multicellular hairs secreting a sticky non-digestive resin. Prey is captured but digestion relies entirely on symbiotic bugs. Both species bear attractive pink to mauve flowers and are of significant ecological and evolutionary interest.
genera.meta_description: Buy Roridula online. Rare protocarnivorous Cape fynbos shrubs with resin-trap leaves. Seedlings and TC stock with full care information.
genera.meta_keywords: Roridula for sale, fly bush, fynbos carnivore, Roridula gorgonias, protocarnivorous shrub
genera.native_range: Western Cape, South Africa (Cape Floristic Region)

species.description: Roridula gorgonias is the smaller of the two species, reaching 50–80 cm. Leaves are linear to lanceolate with densely resinous tips. Flowers are pink to magenta. Naturally occurs on nutrient-poor sandstone soils in the Cape mountains. Requires fynbos-type conditions: excellent drainage, high light, low humidity, and mineral-poor growing medium.
species.meta_title: Roridula gorgonias — Small Cape Fly Bush
species.meta_description: Buy Roridula gorgonias, the small protocarnivorous fly bush from South Africa. Fynbos native; excellent drainage and full sun essential.
species.meta_keywords: Roridula gorgonias, small fly bush, Cape fynbos plant, protocarnivorous shrub
species.native_range: Western Cape, South Africa (Cederberg to Hottentots Holland)
species.notes: Fynbos conditions: sandy, acidic, nutrient-poor, summer-dry. Very difficult in cultivation.

species.description: Roridula dentata is the larger species, growing to 1.5 m as a multi-stemmed shrub. Leaves are broader and more distinctly dentate than R. gorgonias. Flowers are pale pink to white. Equally specific in cultivation requirements; the most-studied species in terms of its protocarnivorous symbiosis with Pameridea roridulae.
species.meta_title: Roridula dentata — Large Cape Fly Bush
species.meta_description: Buy Roridula dentata, the large toothed-leaf fly bush. Studies with Pameridea symbiosis; rare Cape fynbos specimen for advanced growers.
species.meta_keywords: Roridula dentata, large fly bush, Pameridea symbiosis, Cape fynbos shrub
species.native_range: Western Cape, South Africa (Caledon to Bredasdorp)
species.notes: Taller and more robust than R. gorgonias. Same strict fynbos cultural requirements.
